title: client.rs TransportInner::comm_pointers: three identical match arms
description: In client.rs, TransportInner::comm_pointers has three match arms that are structurally identical: each extracts (&inner.transport_cb, &mut inner.transport_ctx as *mut _ as *mut c_void, &inner.transport_cfg as *const _ as *const c_void). The only difference is the concrete type behind the pointer. Because the fields share names (transport_cb, transport_ctx, transport_cfg) across all three Inner structs, a helper macro or a shared trait would eliminate the repetition. Alternatively, extracting a per-variant 'fn as_comm_pointers(&mut self) -> (...)' method on each Inner struct (via a local trait CommPtrs) makes the pattern explicit: impl CommPtrs for TcpInner { fn comm_pointers(&mut self) -> ... { ... } } and then the match arm is just inner.comm_pointers(). This avoids the three-way copy-paste while preserving the unsafe pointer extraction in one place.
